mainEntity.name: What if the accident was my fault—can I still get paid if I can't work?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Workers' comp is a no-fault system. Unless you were intoxicated or intentionally hurt yourself, you are entitled to full benefits even if the accident was caused by your own mistake.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Utah and Idaho are 'no-fault' workers' compensation states. This means even if you were clumsy, made a mistake, or were partially responsible for the accident that left you unable to work, you are still entitled to 100% of your medical and wage benefits. The only common exceptions are if you were intoxicated on the job or intentionally injured yourself.

Being 'at fault' does not reduce your weekly check or your medical coverage.

- No-fault coverage is the trade-off for not being able to sue your boss for pain and suffering.
- Intoxication or 'horseplay' can sometimes lead to a claim denial.
- Safety rule violations can occasionally result in a 15% reduction in benefits in some states.

Do not let your employer or an adjuster make you feel guilty or tell you that 'because it was your fault' you don't get paid. This is a common tactic to discourage filing claims. Just Work Comp Law focuses on protecting these rights.
